TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int, boolean, boolean, boolean, int, int)\n     */\n    public static void writeEmptyHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                        byte magic,\n                                        long producerId,\n                                        short producerEpoch,\n                                        int baseSequence,\n                                        long baseOffset,\n                                        long lastOffset,\n                                        int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                        TimestampType timestampType,\n                                        long timestamp,\n                                        boolean isTransactional,\n                                        boolean isControlRecord) {\n        int offsetDelta = (int) (lastOffset - baseOffset);\n        writeHeader(buffer, baseOffset, offsetDelta, DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD, magic,\n                    CompressionType.NONE, timestampType, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, timestamp, producerId,\n                    producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, isControlRecord, false, partitionLeaderEpoch, 0);\n    }\n\n    public static void writeHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                   long baseOffset,\n                                   int lastOffsetDelta,\n                                   int sizeInBytes,\n                                   byte magic,\n                                   CompressionType compressionType,\n                                   TimestampType timestampType,\n                                   long baseTimestamp,\n                                   long maxTimestamp,\n                                   long producerId,\n                                   short epoch,\n                                   int sequence,\n                                   boolean isTransactional,\n                                   boolean isControlBatch,\n                                   boolean isDeleteHorizonSet,\n                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                   int numRecords) {\n        if (magic < RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid magic value \" + magic);\n        if (baseTimestamp < 0 && baseTimestamp != NO_TIMESTAMP)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid message timestamp \" + baseTimestamp);\n\n        short attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType, timestampType, isTransactional, isControlBatch, isDeleteHorizonSet);\n\n        int position = buffer.position();\n        buffer.putLong(position + BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, baseOffset);\n        buffer.putInt(position + LENGTH_OFFSET, sizeInBytes - LOG_OVERHEAD);\n        buffer.putInt(position + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n        buffer.put(position + MAGIC_OFFSET, magic);\n        buffer.putShort(position + ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\n        buffer.putLong(position + BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, baseTimestamp);\n        buffer.putLong(position + MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\n        buffer.putInt(position + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET, lastOffsetDelta);\n        buffer.putLong(position + PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET, producerId);\n        buffer.putShort(position + PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\n        buffer.putInt(position + BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET, sequence);\n        buffer.putInt(position + RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET, numRecords);\n        long crc = Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, sizeInBytes - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n        buffer.putInt(position + CRC_OFFSET, (int) crc);\n        buffer.position(position + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"RecordBatch(magic=\" + magic() + \", offsets=[\" + baseOffset() + \", \" + lastOffset() + \"], \" +\n                \"sequence=[\" + baseSequence() + \", \" + lastSequence() + \"], \" +\n                \"partitionLeaderEpoch=\" + partitionLeaderEpoch() + \", \" +\n                \"isTransactional=\" + isTransactional() + \", isControlBatch=\" + isControlBatch() + \", \" +\n                \"compression=\" + compressionType() + \", timestampType=\" + timestampType() + \", crc=\" + checksum() + \")\";\n    }\n\n    public static int sizeInBytes(long baseOffset, Iterable<Record> records) {\n        Iterator<Record> iterator = records.iterator();\n        if (!iterator.hasNext())\n            return 0;\n\n        int size = RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n        Long baseTimestamp = null;\n        while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n            Record record = iterator.next();\n            int offsetDelta = (int) (record.offset() - baseOffset);\n            if (baseTimestamp == null)\n                baseTimestamp = record.timestamp();\n            long timestampDelta = record.timestamp() - baseTimestamp;\n            size += DefaultRecord.sizeInBytes(offsetDelta, timestampDelta, record.key(), record.value(),\n                    record.headers());\n        }\n        return size;\n    }\n\n    public static int sizeInBytes(Iterable<SimpleRecord> records) {\n        Iterator<SimpleRecord> iterator = records.iterator();\n        if (!iterator.hasNext())\n            return 0;\n\n        int size = RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n        int offsetDelta = 0;\n        Long baseTimestamp = null;\n        while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n            SimpleRecord record = iterator.next();\n            if (baseTimestamp == null)\n                baseTimestamp = record.timestamp();\n            long timestampDelta = record.timestamp() - baseTimestamp;\n            size += DefaultRecord.sizeInBytes(offsetDelta++, timestampDelta, record.key(), record.value(),\n                    record.headers());\n        }\n        return size;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value. This\n     * is only an estimate because it does not take into account additional overhead from the compression\n     * algorithm used.\n     */\n    static int estimateBatchSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer value, Header[] headers) {\n        return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + DefaultRecord.recordSizeUpperBound(key, value, headers);\n    }\n\n    public static int incrementSequence(int sequence, int increment) {\n        if (sequence > Integer.MAX_VALUE - increment)\n            return increment - (Integer.MAX_VALUE - sequence) - 1;\n        return sequence + increment;\n    }\n\n    public static int decrementSequence(int sequence, int decrement) {\n        if (sequence < decrement)\n            return Integer.MAX_VALUE - (decrement - sequence) + 1;\n        return sequence - decrement;\n    }\n\n    // visible for testing\n    abstract class RecordIterator implements CloseableIterator<Record> {\n        private final Long logAppendTime;\n        private final long baseOffset;\n        private final long baseTimestamp;\n        private final int baseSequence;\n        private final int numRecords;\n        private int readRecords = 0;\n\n        RecordIterator() {\n            this.logAppendTime = timestampType() == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ? maxTimestamp() : null;\n            this.baseOffset = baseOffset();\n            this.baseTimestamp = baseTimestamp();\n            this.baseSequence = baseSequence();\n            int numRecords = count();\n            if (numRecords < 0)\n                throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Found invalid record count \" + numRecords + \" in magic v\" +\n                        magic() + \" batch\");\n            this.numRecords = numRecords;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean hasNext() {\n            return readRecords < numRecords;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public Record next() {\n            if (readRecords >= numRecords)\n                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n\n            readRecords++;\n            Record rec = readNext(baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n            if (readRecords == numRecords) {\n                // Validate that the actual size of the batch is equal to declared size\n                // by checking that after reading declared number of items, there no items left\n                // (overflow case, i.e. reading past buffer end is checked elsewhere).\n                if (!ensureNoneRemaining())\n                    throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, records still remaining in file\");\n            }\n            return rec;\n        }\n\n        protected abstract Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime);\n\n        protected abstract boolean ensureNoneRemaining();\n\n        @Override\n        public void remove() {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException();\n        }\n\n    }\n\n    // visible for testing\n    abstract class StreamRecordIterator extends RecordIterator {\n        private final InputStream inputStream;\n\n        StreamRecordIterator(InputStream inputStream) {\n            super();\n            this.inputStream = inputStream;\n        }\n\n        abstract Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException;\n\n        @Override\n        protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\n            try {\n                return doReadRecord(baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n            } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\n                throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, premature EOF reached\", e);\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to decompress record stream\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected boolean ensureNoneRemaining() {\n            try {\n                return inputStream.read() == -1;\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Error checking for remaining bytes after reading batch\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() {\n            try {\n                inputStream.close();\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to close record stream\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    static class DefaultFileChannelRecordBatch extends FileLogInputStream.FileChannelRecordBatch {\n\n        DefaultFileChannelRecordBatch(long offset,\n                                      byte magic,\n                                      FileRecords fileRecords,\n                                      int position,\n                                      int batchSize) {\n            super(offset, magic, fileRecords, position, batchSize);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected RecordBatch toMemoryRecordBatch(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n            return new DefaultRecordBatch(buffer);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long baseOffset() {\n            return offset;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long lastOffset() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().lastOffset();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long producerId() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().producerId();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public short producerEpoch() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().producerEpoch();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int baseSequence() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().baseSequence();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int lastSequence() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().lastSequence();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public Integer countOrNull() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().countOrNull();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean isTransactional() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().isTransactional();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().deleteHorizonMs();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean isControlBatch() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().isControlBatch();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int partitionLeaderEpoch() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().partitionLeaderEpoch();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected int headerSize() {\n            return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n        }\n    }\n\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "                     producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, isControlRecord, false, partitionLeaderEpoch, 0);",
        "     }",
        " ",
        "+    /**",
        "+     * Writes a record batch header to the buffer with the specified parameters.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p>This method writes the complete batch header (including all fields) and computes the CRC-32C checksum",
        "+     * over the batch. After writing, the buffer position is advanced to the end of the header, ready for writing",
        "+     * record data.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p><b>Batch Header Format (for magic v2):</b>",
        "+     * <pre>",
        "+     * | BaseOffset (8) | Length (4) | PartitionLeaderEpoch (4) | Magic (1) |",
        "+     * | CRC (4) | Attributes (2) | LastOffsetDelta (4) | BaseTimestamp (8) |",
        "+     * | MaxTimestamp (8) | ProducerId (8) | ProducerEpoch (2) | BaseSequence (4) |",
        "+     * | RecordsCount (4) | [Records] |",
        "+     * </pre>",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p><b>CRC Computation:</b> The CRC covers from the Attributes field through the end of the batch",
        "+     * (including record data). The PartitionLeaderEpoch is explicitly excluded to avoid recomputation",
        "+     * when the epoch is updated after receipt by a broker.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer to write to. Position should be at the start of the header.",
        "+     *               Must have at least sizeInBytes bytes remaining.",
        "+     * @param baseOffset The offset of the first record in the batch",
        "+     * @param lastOffsetDelta The offset delta of the last record (lastOffset - baseOffset)",
        "+     * @param sizeInBytes Total batch size including the base offset and length fields ({@code RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + records size})",
        "+     * @param magic The magic version (must be {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE})",
        "+     * @param compressionType The compression algorithm used for records (NONE, GZIP, SNAPPY, LZ4, ZSTD)",
        "+     * @param timestampType The timestamp type (CREATE_TIME or LOG_APPEND_TIME)",
        "+     * @param baseTimestamp Base timestamp for record timestamp deltas, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_TIMESTAMP}",
        "+     * @param maxTimestamp Maximum timestamp in the batch (or delete horizon if delete horizon flag is set)",
        "+     * @param producerId Producer ID for idempotent/transactional batches, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_PRODUCER_ID}",
        "+     * @param epoch Producer epoch, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH}",
        "+     * @param sequence Base sequence number, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_SEQUENCE}",
        "+     * @param isTransactional Whether this batch is part of a transaction",
        "+     * @param isControlBatch Whether this batch contains control records",
        "+     * @param isDeleteHorizonSet Whether the delete horizon flag is set (baseTimestamp is delete horizon)",
        "+     * @param partitionLeaderEpoch The partition leader epoch at the broker (not included in CRC)",
        "+     * @param numRecords The number of records in this batch",
        "+     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if magic < {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE} or timestamp is invalid",
        "+     * @see #writeEmptyHeader(ByteBuffer, byte, long, short, int, long, long, int, TimestampType, long, boolean, boolean)",
        "+     * @see CompressionType for compression type details",
        "+     */",
        "     public static void writeHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,",
        "                                    long baseOffset,",
        "                                    int lastOffsetDelta,"
      ],
      "newLines": 47,
      "newStart": 573,
      "oldLines": 6,
      "oldStart": 573
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}
18. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java",
  "new_string": "    /**\n     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value.\n     *\n     * <p>This is only an estimate and does not take into account:\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Additional overhead from the compression algorithm used (e.g., frame headers for LZ4)</li>\n     *   <li>Memory padding or alignment</li>\n     *   <li>Stream format overhead for compressed batches</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Use Case:</b> Useful for buffer pre-allocation to avoid reallocation during batch building.\n     *\n     * @param key The record key, or null for null keys\n     * @param value The record value, or null for null values\n     * @param headers The record headers, or empty array for no headers\n     * @return An upper bound on the serialized size in bytes. Actual size may be smaller due to compression.\n     * @see DefaultRecord#recordSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer, ByteBuffer, Header[])\n     */\n    static int estimateBatchSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer value, Header[] headers) {\n        return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + DefaultRecord.recordSizeUpperBound(key, value, headers);\n    }",
  "old_string": "    /**\n     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value. This\n     * is only an estimate because it does not take into account additional overhead from the compression\n     * algorithm used.\n     */\n    static int estimateBatchSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer value, Header[] headers) {\n        return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + DefaultRecord.recordSizeUpperBound(key, value, headers);\n    }",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value.\n     *\n     * <p>This is only an estimate and does not take into account:\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Additional overhead from the compression algorithm used (e.g., frame headers for LZ4)</li>\n     *   <li>Memory padding or alignment</li>\n     *   <li>Stream format overhead for compressed batches</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Use Case:</b> Useful for buffer pre-allocation to avoid reallocation during batch building.\n     *\n     * @param key The record key, or null for null keys\n     * @param value The record value, or null for null values\n     * @param headers The record headers, or empty array for no headers\n     * @return An upper bound on the serialized size in bytes. Actual size may be smaller due to compression.\n     * @see DefaultRecord#recordSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer, ByteBuffer, Header[])\n     */\n    static int estimateBatchSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer value, Header[] headers) {\n        return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + DefaultRecord.recordSizeUpperBound(key, value, headers);\n    }",
  "oldString": "    /**\n     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value. This\n     * is only an estimate because it does not take into account additional overhead from the compression\n     * algorithm used.\n     */\n    static int estimateBatchSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer value, Header[] headers) {\n        return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + DefaultRecord.recordSizeUpperBound(key, value, headers);\n    }",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.InvalidRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.header.Header;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteUtils;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Crc32C;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.io.InputStream;\nimport java.nio.BufferUnderflowException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Collections;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.NoSuchElementException;\nimport java.util.Objects;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\nimport static org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.Records.LOG_OVERHEAD;\n\n/**\n * RecordBatch implementation for magic 2 and above. The schema is given below:\n *\n * RecordBatch =>\n *  BaseOffset => Int64\n *  Length => Int32\n *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32\n *  Magic => Int8\n *  CRC => Uint32\n *  Attributes => Int16\n *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32 // also serves as LastSequenceDelta\n *  BaseTimestamp => Int64\n *  MaxTimestamp => Int64\n *  ProducerId => Int64\n *  ProducerEpoch => Int16\n *  BaseSequence => Int32\n *  RecordsCount => Int32\n *  Records => [Record]\n *\n * Note that when compression is enabled (see attributes below), the compressed record data is serialized\n * directly following the count of the number of records.\n *\n * The CRC covers the data from the attributes to the end of the batch (i.e. all the bytes that follow the CRC). It is\n * located after the magic byte, which means that clients must parse the magic byte before deciding how to interpret\n * the bytes between the batch length and the magic byte. The partition leader epoch field is not included in the CRC\n * computation to avoid the need to recompute the CRC when this field is assigned for every batch that is received by\n * the broker. The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial is used for the computation.\n *\n * On Compaction: Unlike the older message formats, magic v2 and above preserves the first and last offset/sequence\n * numbers from the original batch when the log is cleaned. This is required in order to be able to restore the\n * producer's state when the log is reloaded. If we did not retain the last sequence number, then following\n * a partition leader failure, once the new leader has rebuilt the producer state from the log, the next sequence\n * expected number would no longer be in sync with what was written by the client. This would cause an\n * unexpected OutOfOrderSequence error, which is typically fatal. The base sequence number must be preserved for\n * duplicate checking: the broker checks incoming Produce requests for duplicates by verifying that the first and\n * last sequence numbers of the incoming batch match the last from that producer.\n *\n * Note that if all of the records in a batch are removed during compaction, the broker may still retain an empty\n * batch header in order to preserve the producer sequence information as described above. These empty batches\n * are retained only until either a new sequence number is written by the corresponding producer or the producerId\n * is expired from lack of activity.\n *\n * There is no similar need to preserve the timestamp from the original batch after compaction. The BaseTimestamp\n * field therefore reflects the timestamp of the first record in the batch in most cases. If the batch is empty, the\n * BaseTimestamp will be set to -1 (NO_TIMESTAMP). If the delete horizon flag is set to 1, the BaseTimestamp\n * will be set to the time at which tombstone records and aborted transaction markers in the batch should be removed.\n *\n * Similarly, the MaxTimestamp field reflects the maximum timestamp of the current records if the timestamp type\n * is CREATE_TIME. For LOG_APPEND_TIME, on the other hand, the MaxTimestamp field reflects the timestamp set\n * by the broker and is preserved after compaction. Additionally, the MaxTimestamp of an empty batch always retains\n * the previous value prior to becoming empty.\n *\n * The current attributes are given below:\n *\n *  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n *  | Unused (7-15) | Delete Horizon Flag (6) | Control (5) | Transactional (4) | Timestamp Type (3) | Compression Type (0-2) |\n *  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n */\npublic class DefaultRecordBatch extends AbstractRecordBatch implements MutableRecordBatch {\n    static final int BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET = 0;\n    static final int BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int LENGTH_OFFSET = BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET + BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH;\n    static final int LENGTH_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET = LENGTH_OFFSET + LENGTH_LENGTH;\n    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int MAGIC_OFFSET = PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n    static final int MAGIC_LENGTH = 1;\n    public static final int CRC_OFFSET = MAGIC_OFFSET + MAGIC_LENGTH;\n    static final int CRC_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET = CRC_OFFSET + CRC_LENGTH;\n    static final int ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH = 2;\n    public static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET = ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET + ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH;\n    static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH;\n    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET = MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n    static final int PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET = PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET + PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH;\n    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 2;\n    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET = PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 4;\n    public static final int RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET = BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET + BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH;\n    static final int RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int RECORDS_OFFSET = RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET + RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH;\n    public static final int RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD = RECORDS_OFFSET;\n\n    private static final byte COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK = 0x07;\n    private static final byte TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK = 0x10;\n    private static final int CONTROL_FLAG_MASK = 0x20;\n    private static final byte DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK = 0x40;\n    private static final byte TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK = 0x08;\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a record batch from the given buffer containing batch header and records.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Thread-Safety:</b> This implementation is not thread-safe. Concurrent access to the underlying\n     * ByteBuffer by multiple threads will result in undefined behavior. If thread-safety is required, synchronize\n     * externally or use immutable copies.\n     *\n     * @param buffer A ByteBuffer positioned at the start of a magic v2+ record batch. The buffer must contain\n     *               a valid batch header of at least {@link #RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD} bytes.\n     */\n    DefaultRecordBatch(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte magic() {\n        return buffer.get(MAGIC_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void ensureValid() {\n        if (sizeInBytes() < RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD)\n            throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record batch is corrupt (the size \" + sizeInBytes() +\n                    \" is smaller than the minimum allowed overhead \" + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + \")\");\n\n        if (!isValid())\n            throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record is corrupt (stored crc = \" + checksum()\n                    + \", computed crc = \" + computeChecksum() + \")\");\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Gets the base timestamp of the batch which is used to calculate the record timestamps from the deltas.\n     *\n     * <p>In the magic v2+ format, individual record timestamps are stored as deltas relative to this base\n     * timestamp to save space. For CREATE_TIME timestamp type, this is typically the timestamp of the first\n     * record. For LOG_APPEND_TIME, this is the timestamp assigned by the broker. If the batch becomes empty after\n     * compaction, this field is set to {@link RecordBatch#NO_TIMESTAMP} (-1).\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance:</b> This method is O(1) and reads directly from the buffer.\n     *\n     * @return The base timestamp in milliseconds since Unix epoch, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_TIMESTAMP} if\n     *         the batch is empty or the timestamp is undefined.\n     * @see #maxTimestamp() for the maximum timestamp in the batch\n     * @see TimestampType#CREATE_TIME\n     */\n    public long baseTimestamp() {\n        return buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long maxTimestamp() {\n        return buffer.getLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public TimestampType timestampType() {\n        return (attributes() & TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK) == 0 ? TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long baseOffset() {\n        return buffer.getLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long lastOffset() {\n        return baseOffset() + lastOffsetDelta();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long producerId() {\n        return buffer.getLong(PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public short producerEpoch() {\n        return buffer.getShort(PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int baseSequence() {\n        return buffer.getInt(BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    private int lastOffsetDelta() {\n        return buffer.getInt(LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int lastSequence() {\n        int baseSequence = baseSequence();\n        if (baseSequence == RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE)\n            return RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE;\n        return incrementSequence(baseSequence, lastOffsetDelta());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public CompressionType compressionType() {\n        return CompressionType.forId(attributes() & COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return LOG_OVERHEAD + buffer.getInt(LENGTH_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    private int count() {\n        return buffer.getInt(RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Integer countOrNull() {\n        return count();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        buffer.put(this.buffer.duplicate());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream outputStream) {\n        outputStream.write(this.buffer.duplicate());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isTransactional() {\n        return (attributes() & TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n    }\n\n    private boolean hasDeleteHorizonMs() {\n        return (attributes() & DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs() {\n        if (hasDeleteHorizonMs())\n            return OptionalLong.of(buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET));\n        else\n            return OptionalLong.empty();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isControlBatch() {\n        return (attributes() & CONTROL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int partitionLeaderEpoch() {\n        return buffer.getInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Creates an input stream for reading the records in this batch, handling decompression if necessary.\n     *\n     * <p>This method handles the decompression setup for compressed batches. For uncompressed batches, it simply\n     * wraps the buffer. For compressed batches, it returns a decompression stream.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance Note:</b> The returned stream does not decompress data until it is actually read.\n     * For large compressed batches, this defers memory allocation and CPU cost until records are accessed.\n     *\n     * @param bufferSupplier Supplies buffers for decompression. For optimal performance with reusable buffers,\n     *                       use {@link BufferSupplier#GrowableBufferSupplier} or similar.\n     * @return An InputStream positioned at the start of the records section, with decompression applied if needed.\n     * @throws KafkaException if the compression codec is not available or decompression setup fails\n     */\n    public InputStream recordInputStream(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n        final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\n        buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\n        return Compression.of(compressionType()).build().wrapForInput(buffer, magic(), bufferSupplier);\n    }\n\n    private CloseableIterator<Record> compressedIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier, boolean skipKeyValue) {\n        final InputStream inputStream = recordInputStream(bufferSupplier);\n\n        if (skipKeyValue) {\n            return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\n                @Override\n                protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\n                    return DefaultRecord.readPartiallyFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n                }\n            };\n        } else {\n            return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\n                @Override\n                protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\n                    return DefaultRecord.readFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n                }\n            };\n        }\n    }\n\n    private CloseableIterator<Record> uncompressedIterator() {\n        final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\n        buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\n        return new RecordIterator() {\n            @Override\n            protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\n                try {\n                    return DefaultRecord.readFrom(buffer, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n                } catch (BufferUnderflowException e) {\n                    throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, premature EOF reached\");\n                }\n            }\n            @Override\n            protected boolean ensureNoneRemaining() {\n                return !buffer.hasRemaining();\n            }\n            @Override\n            public void close() {}\n        };\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns an iterator over the records in this batch.\n     *\n     * <p>For uncompressed batches, this returns a lazy iterator that reads records on-demand from the buffer.\n     * For compressed batches, the entire batch is decompressed upfront and returned as an in-memory list iterator.\n     * This ensures that decompression resources can be properly released without relying on explicit close() calls.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Memory Characteristics:</b>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Uncompressed: Minimal memory overhead, lazy decoding</li>\n     *   <li>Compressed: Full decompression into memory required; memory usage = O(decompressed batch size)</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Resource Management:</b> For compressed batches, all decompression happens during this method call.\n     * No explicit resource cleanup is required. For lower memory overhead with large compressed batches, use\n     * {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)} instead.\n     *\n     * @return An Iterator over the records in this batch. For empty batches, returns an empty iterator.\n     * @throws InvalidRecordException if the batch is corrupt or has incorrect declared size\n     * @see #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier) for memory-efficient iteration of compressed batches\n     */\n    @Override\n    public Iterator<Record> iterator() {\n        if (count() == 0)\n            return Collections.emptyIterator();\n\n        if (!isCompressed())\n            return uncompressedIterator();\n\n        // for a normal iterator, we cannot ensure that the underlying compression stream is closed,\n        // so we decompress the full record set here. Use cases which call for a lower memory footprint\n        // can use `streamingIterator` at the cost of additional complexity\n        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = compressedIterator(BufferSupplier.NO_CACHING, false)) {\n            List<Record> records = new ArrayList<>(count());\n            while (iterator.hasNext())\n                records.add(iterator.next());\n            return records.iterator();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns a closeable iterator that skips key, value, and header fields for memory efficiency.\n     *\n     * <p>This method is useful for scenarios where only record metadata (offset, timestamp, etc.) is needed\n     * and record payloads can be skipped. For uncompressed batches, actual skipping is not performed since\n     * buffer skip operations are less efficient than simply reading the data. For compressed batches, payload\n     * skipping is performed during decompression.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Memory Efficiency:</b> For compressed batches with large payloads, this can significantly reduce\n     * memory consumption by avoiding full decompression of record keys and values. For uncompressed batches,\n     * the memory savings are negligible since all data must be read anyway.\n     *\n     * @param bufferSupplier Supplies buffers for decompression in compressed batches\n     * @return A closeable iterator over records with payloads skipped. Callers must close this iterator to\n     *         release decompression resources, especially important for compressed batches.\n     * @see #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier) for full streaming iteration\n     * @see #iterator() for standard iteration\n     */\n    @Override\n    public CloseableIterator<Record> skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n        if (count() == 0) {\n            return CloseableIterator.wrap(Collections.emptyIterator());\n        }\n\n        /*\n         * For uncompressed iterator, it is actually not worth skipping key / value / headers at all since\n         * its ByteBufferInputStream's skip() function is less efficient compared with just reading it actually\n         * as it will allocate new byte array.\n         */\n        if (!isCompressed())\n            return uncompressedIterator();\n\n        // we define this to be a closable iterator so that caller (i.e. the log validator) needs to close it\n        // while we can save memory footprint of not decompressing the full record set ahead of time\n        return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, true);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns a closeable iterator with lazy decompression of records.\n     *\n     * <p>For uncompressed batches, this is equivalent to {@link #iterator()} but returns a closeable iterator.\n     * For compressed batches, decompression is deferred until records are actually requested via iteration,\n     * reducing memory overhead for batches where not all records are consumed.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Thread-Safety:</b> The returned iterator is not thread-safe. The underlying decompression state\n     * is managed by the iterator, and concurrent access will result in undefined behavior.\n     *\n     * @param bufferSupplier Supplies buffers for decompression. Reusable suppliers (e.g.,\n     *                       {@link BufferSupplier#GrowableBufferSupplier}) significantly improve performance\n     *                       for repeated iterations or multiple batches.\n     * @return A closeable iterator over all records in this batch with lazy decompression. Callers must close\n     *         this iterator to release decompression resources.\n     * @see #skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier) for memory-efficient metadata-only iteration\n     * @see #iterator() for standard iteration that decompresses upfront for compressed batches\n     */\n    @Override\n    public CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n        if (isCompressed())\n            return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, false);\n        else\n            return uncompressedIterator();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setLastOffset(long offset) {\n        buffer.putLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, offset - lastOffsetDelta());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType timestampType, long maxTimestamp) {\n        long currentMaxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n        // We don't need to recompute crc if the timestamp is not updated.\n        if (timestampType() == timestampType && currentMaxTimestamp == maxTimestamp)\n            return;\n\n        byte attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType(), timestampType, isTransactional(), isControlBatch(), hasDeleteHorizonMs());\n        buffer.putShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\n        buffer.putLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\n        long crc = computeChecksum();\n        ByteUtils.writeUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET, crc);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setPartitionLeaderEpoch(int epoch) {\n        buffer.putInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long checksum() {\n        return ByteUtils.readUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    public boolean isValid() {\n        return sizeInBytes() >= RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD && checksum() == computeChecksum();\n    }\n\n    private long computeChecksum() {\n        return Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, buffer.limit() - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    private byte attributes() {\n        // note we're not using the second byte of attributes\n        return (byte) buffer.getShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n        if (this == o)\n            return true;\n        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n            return false;\n\n        DefaultRecordBatch that = (DefaultRecordBatch) o;\n        return Objects.equals(buffer, that.buffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int hashCode() {\n        return buffer != null ? buffer.hashCode() : 0;\n    }\n\n    private static byte computeAttributes(CompressionType type, TimestampType timestampType,\n                                          boolean isTransactional, boolean isControl, boolean isDeleteHorizonSet) {\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Timestamp type must be provided to compute attributes for message \" +\n                    \"format v2 and above\");\n\n        byte attributes = isTransactional ? TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK : 0;\n        if (isControl)\n            attributes |= CONTROL_FLAG_MASK;\n        if (type.id > 0)\n            attributes |= (byte) (COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK & type.id);\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            attributes |= TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK;\n        if (isDeleteHorizonSet)\n            attributes |= DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK;\n        return attributes;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Writes an empty record batch header to the buffer.\n     *\n     * <p>Empty batches are used to preserve producer state information (base sequence, last sequence) after\n     * log compaction, even when all records have been removed. This allows brokers to maintain idempotence\n     * and transaction state correctly.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance:</b> This method writes {@link #RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD} bytes to the buffer.\n     * The CRC is computed over the batch header and record data (none for empty batches).\n     *\n     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer to write to. Must have at least {@link #RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD} bytes remaining.\n     * @param magic The magic version (must be {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE})\n     * @param producerId The producer ID for idempotent/transactional producers, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_PRODUCER_ID}\n     * @param producerEpoch The producer epoch, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH}\n     * @param baseSequence The base sequence number, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_SEQUENCE}\n     * @param baseOffset The offset of this batch\n     * @param lastOffset The last offset in the original batch (preserved for compaction)\n     * @param partitionLeaderEpoch The partition leader epoch, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH}\n     * @param timestampType The timestamp type (CREATE_TIME or LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n     * @param timestamp The timestamp for this batch (max timestamp or delete horizon)\n     * @param isTransactional Whether this batch is part of a transaction\n     * @param isControlRecord Whether this is a control record batch\n     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if magic version is invalid or timestamp is invalid\n     * @see #writeHeader(ByteBuffer, long, int, int, byte, CompressionType, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int, boolean, boolean, boolean, int, int)\n     */\n    public static void writeEmptyHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                        byte magic,\n                                        long producerId,\n                                        short producerEpoch,\n                                        int baseSequence,\n                                        long baseOffset,\n                                        long lastOffset,\n                                        int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                        TimestampType timestampType,\n                                        long timestamp,\n                                        boolean isTransactional,\n                                        boolean isControlRecord) {\n        int offsetDelta = (int) (lastOffset - baseOffset);\n        writeHeader(buffer, baseOffset, offsetDelta, DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD, magic,\n                    CompressionType.NONE, timestampType, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, timestamp, producerId,\n                    producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, isControlRecord, false, partitionLeaderEpoch, 0);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Writes a record batch header to the buffer with the specified parameters.\n     *\n     * <p>This method writes the complete batch header (including all fields) and computes the CRC-32C checksum\n     * over the batch. After writing, the buffer position is advanced to the end of the header, ready for writing\n     * record data.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Batch Header Format (for magic v2):</b>\n     * <pre>\n     * | BaseOffset (8) | Length (4) | PartitionLeaderEpoch (4) | Magic (1) |\n     * | CRC (4) | Attributes (2) | LastOffsetDelta (4) | BaseTimestamp (8) |\n     * | MaxTimestamp (8) | ProducerId (8) | ProducerEpoch (2) | BaseSequence (4) |\n     * | RecordsCount (4) | [Records] |\n     * </pre>\n     *\n     * <p><b>CRC Computation:</b> The CRC covers from the Attributes field through the end of the batch\n     * (including record data). The PartitionLeaderEpoch is explicitly excluded to avoid recomputation\n     * when the epoch is updated after receipt by a broker.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer to write to. Position should be at the start of the header.\n     *               Must have at least sizeInBytes bytes remaining.\n     * @param baseOffset The offset of the first record in the batch\n     * @param lastOffsetDelta The offset delta of the last record (lastOffset - baseOffset)\n     * @param sizeInBytes Total batch size including the base offset and length fields ({@code RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + records size})\n     * @param magic The magic version (must be {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE})\n     * @param compressionType The compression algorithm used for records (NONE, GZIP, SNAPPY, LZ4, ZSTD)\n     * @param timestampType The timestamp type (CREATE_TIME or LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n     * @param baseTimestamp Base timestamp for record timestamp deltas, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_TIMESTAMP}\n     * @param maxTimestamp Maximum timestamp in the batch (or delete horizon if delete horizon flag is set)\n     * @param producerId Producer ID for idempotent/transactional batches, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_PRODUCER_ID}\n     * @param epoch Producer epoch, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH}\n     * @param sequence Base sequence number, or {@link RecordBatch#NO_SEQUENCE}\n     * @param isTransactional Whether this batch is part of a transaction\n     * @param isControlBatch Whether this batch contains control records\n     * @param isDeleteHorizonSet Whether the delete horizon flag is set (baseTimestamp is delete horizon)\n     * @param partitionLeaderEpoch The partition leader epoch at the broker (not included in CRC)\n     * @param numRecords The number of records in this batch\n     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if magic < {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE} or timestamp is invalid\n     * @see #writeEmptyHeader(ByteBuffer, byte, long, short, int, long, long, int, TimestampType, long, boolean, boolean)\n     * @see CompressionType for compression type details\n     */\n    public static void writeHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                   long baseOffset,\n                                   int lastOffsetDelta,\n                                   int sizeInBytes,\n                                   byte magic,\n                                   CompressionType compressionType,\n                                   TimestampType timestampType,\n                                   long baseTimestamp,\n                                   long maxTimestamp,\n                                   long producerId,\n                                   short epoch,\n                                   int sequence,\n                                   boolean isTransactional,\n                                   boolean isControlBatch,\n                                   boolean isDeleteHorizonSet,\n                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                   int numRecords) {\n        if (magic < RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid magic value \" + magic);\n        if (baseTimestamp < 0 && baseTimestamp != NO_TIMESTAMP)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid message timestamp \" + baseTimestamp);\n\n        short attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType, timestampType, isTransactional, isControlBatch, isDeleteHorizonSet);\n\n        int position = buffer.position();\n        buffer.putLong(position + BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, baseOffset);\n        buffer.putInt(position + LENGTH_OFFSET, sizeInBytes - LOG_OVERHEAD);\n        buffer.putInt(position + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n        buffer.put(position + MAGIC_OFFSET, magic);\n        buffer.putShort(position + ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\n        buffer.putLong(position + BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, baseTimestamp);\n        buffer.putLong(position + MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\n        buffer.putInt(position + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET, lastOffsetDelta);\n        buffer.putLong(position + PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET, producerId);\n        buffer.putShort(position + PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\n        buffer.putInt(position + BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET, sequence);\n        buffer.putInt(position + RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET, numRecords);\n        long crc = Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, sizeInBytes - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n        buffer.putInt(position + CRC_OFFSET, (int) crc);\n        buffer.position(position + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"RecordBatch(magic=\" + magic() + \", offsets=[\" + baseOffset() + \", \" + lastOffset() + \"], \" +\n                \"sequence=[\" + baseSequence() + \", \" + lastSequence() + \"], \" +\n                \"partitionLeaderEpoch=\" + partitionLeaderEpoch() + \", \" +\n                \"isTransactional=\" + isTransactional() + \", isControlBatch=\" + isControlBatch() + \", \" +\n                \"compression=\" + compressionType() + \", timestampType=\" + timestampType() + \", crc=\" + checksum() + \")\";\n    }\n\n    public static int sizeInBytes(long baseOffset, Iterable<Record> records) {\n        Iterator<Record> iterator = records.iterator();\n        if (!iterator.hasNext())\n            return 0;\n\n        int size = RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n        Long baseTimestamp = null;\n        while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n            Record record = iterator.next();\n            int offsetDelta = (int) (record.offset() - baseOffset);\n            if (baseTimestamp == null)\n                baseTimestamp = record.timestamp();\n            long timestampDelta = record.timestamp() - baseTimestamp;\n            size += DefaultRecord.sizeInBytes(offsetDelta, timestampDelta, record.key(), record.value(),\n                    record.headers());\n        }\n        return size;\n    }\n\n    public static int sizeInBytes(Iterable<SimpleRecord> records) {\n        Iterator<SimpleRecord> iterator = records.iterator();\n        if (!iterator.hasNext())\n            return 0;\n\n        int size = RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n        int offsetDelta = 0;\n        Long baseTimestamp = null;\n        while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n            SimpleRecord record = iterator.next();\n            if (baseTimestamp == null)\n                baseTimestamp = record.timestamp();\n            long timestampDelta = record.timestamp() - baseTimestamp;\n            size += DefaultRecord.sizeInBytes(offsetDelta++, timestampDelta, record.key(), record.value(),\n                    record.headers());\n        }\n        return size;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value. This\n     * is only an estimate because it does not take into account additional overhead from the compression\n     * algorithm used.\n     */\n    static int estimateBatchSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer value, Header[] headers) {\n        return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + DefaultRecord.recordSizeUpperBound(key, value, headers);\n    }\n\n    public static int incrementSequence(int sequence, int increment) {\n        if (sequence > Integer.MAX_VALUE - increment)\n            return increment - (Integer.MAX_VALUE - sequence) - 1;\n        return sequence + increment;\n    }\n\n    public static int decrementSequence(int sequence, int decrement) {\n        if (sequence < decrement)\n            return Integer.MAX_VALUE - (decrement - sequence) + 1;\n        return sequence - decrement;\n    }\n\n    // visible for testing\n    abstract class RecordIterator implements CloseableIterator<Record> {\n        private final Long logAppendTime;\n        private final long baseOffset;\n        private final long baseTimestamp;\n        private final int baseSequence;\n        private final int numRecords;\n        private int readRecords = 0;\n\n        RecordIterator() {\n            this.logAppendTime = timestampType() == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ? maxTimestamp() : null;\n            this.baseOffset = baseOffset();\n            this.baseTimestamp = baseTimestamp();\n            this.baseSequence = baseSequence();\n            int numRecords = count();\n            if (numRecords < 0)\n                throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Found invalid record count \" + numRecords + \" in magic v\" +\n                        magic() + \" batch\");\n            this.numRecords = numRecords;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean hasNext() {\n            return readRecords < numRecords;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public Record next() {\n            if (readRecords >= numRecords)\n                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n\n            readRecords++;\n            Record rec = readNext(baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n            if (readRecords == numRecords) {\n                // Validate that the actual size of the batch is equal to declared size\n                // by checking that after reading declared number of items, there no items left\n                // (overflow case, i.e. reading past buffer end is checked elsewhere).\n                if (!ensureNoneRemaining())\n                    throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, records still remaining in file\");\n            }\n            return rec;\n        }\n\n        protected abstract Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime);\n\n        protected abstract boolean ensureNoneRemaining();\n\n        @Override\n        public void remove() {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException();\n        }\n\n    }\n\n    // visible for testing\n    abstract class StreamRecordIterator extends RecordIterator {\n        private final InputStream inputStream;\n\n        StreamRecordIterator(InputStream inputStream) {\n            super();\n            this.inputStream = inputStream;\n        }\n\n        abstract Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException;\n\n        @Override\n        protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\n            try {\n                return doReadRecord(baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n            } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\n                throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, premature EOF reached\", e);\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to decompress record stream\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected boolean ensureNoneRemaining() {\n            try {\n                return inputStream.read() == -1;\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Error checking for remaining bytes after reading batch\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() {\n            try {\n                inputStream.close();\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to close record stream\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    static class DefaultFileChannelRecordBatch extends FileLogInputStream.FileChannelRecordBatch {\n\n        DefaultFileChannelRecordBatch(long offset,\n                                      byte magic,\n                                      FileRecords fileRecords,\n                                      int position,\n                                      int batchSize) {\n            super(offset, magic, fileRecords, position, batchSize);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected RecordBatch toMemoryRecordBatch(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n            return new DefaultRecordBatch(buffer);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long baseOffset() {\n            return offset;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long lastOffset() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().lastOffset();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long producerId() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().producerId();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public short producerEpoch() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().producerEpoch();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int baseSequence() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().baseSequence();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int lastSequence() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().lastSequence();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public Integer countOrNull() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().countOrNull();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean isTransactional() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().isTransactional();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().deleteHorizonMs();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean isControlBatch() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().isControlBatch();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int partitionLeaderEpoch() {\n            return loadBatchHeader().partitionLeaderEpoch();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected int headerSize() {\n            return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n        }\n    }\n\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     }",
        " ",
        "     /**",
        "-     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value. This",
        "-     * is only an estimate because it does not take into account additional overhead from the compression",
        "-     * algorithm used.",
        "+     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p>This is only an estimate and does not take into account:",
        "+     * <ul>",
        "+     *   <li>Additional overhead from the compression algorithm used (e.g., frame headers for LZ4)</li>",
        "+     *   <li>Memory padding or alignment</li>",
        "+     *   <li>Stream format overhead for compressed batches</li>",
        "+     * </ul>",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p><b>Use Case:</b> Useful for buffer pre-allocation to avoid reallocation during batch building.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * @param key The record key, or null for null keys",
        "+     * @param value The record value, or null for null values",
        "+     * @param headers The record headers, or empty array for no headers",
        "+     * @return An upper bound on the serialized size in bytes. Actual size may be smaller due to compression.",
        "+     * @see DefaultRecord#recordSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer, ByteBuffer, Header[])",
        "      */",
        "     static int estimateBatchSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer value, Header[] headers) {",
        "         return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + DefaultRecord.recordSizeUpperBound(key, value, headers);"
      ],

19. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java",
  "new_string": "/**\n * A {@link Records} implementation backed by a ByteBuffer. This is used for reading, writing, and modifying\n * in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer, use {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder}\n * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> MemoryRecords is the primary in-memory representation of serialized Kafka\n * record batches. It provides methods for iterating over batches, filtering records by retention policies,\n * and serializing to various output formats (ByteBuffer, channel, output stream).\n *\n * <p><b>Thread-Safety:</b> This class is not thread-safe. The underlying ByteBuffer is shared with callers, and\n * concurrent modification (e.g., changing the buffer position or limit) will cause undefined behavior. Use external\n * synchronization or create separate instances for concurrent access.\n *\n * <p><b>Memory Efficiency:</b> MemoryRecords provides lazy iteration over batches and lazy decompression of\n * records via {@link RecordBatch#streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}. This allows for processing large record\n * sets with bounded memory usage.\n *\n * <p><b>Filtering and Compaction:</b> The {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)} method\n * supports in-place record filtering for log compaction and tombstone deletion. This method handles batch\n * rebuilding with updated metadata as needed.\n *\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for building new record buffers\n * @see RecordBatch for individual batch interface\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch for magic v2+ concrete implementation\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {",
  "old_string": "/**\n * A {@link Records} implementation backed by a ByteBuffer. This is used only for reading or\n * modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer see {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder},\n * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java",
  "newString": "/**\n * A {@link Records} implementation backed by a ByteBuffer. This is used for reading, writing, and modifying\n * in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer, use {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder}\n * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> MemoryRecords is the primary in-memory representation of serialized Kafka\n * record batches. It provides methods for iterating over batches, filtering records by retention policies,\n * and serializing to various output formats (ByteBuffer, channel, output stream).\n *\n * <p><b>Thread-Safety:</b> This class is not thread-safe. The underlying ByteBuffer is shared with callers, and\n * concurrent modification (e.g., changing the buffer position or limit) will cause undefined behavior. Use external\n * synchronization or create separate instances for concurrent access.\n *\n * <p><b>Memory Efficiency:</b> MemoryRecords provides lazy iteration over batches and lazy decompression of\n * records via {@link RecordBatch#streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}. This allows for processing large record\n * sets with bounded memory usage.\n *\n * <p><b>Filtering and Compaction:</b> The {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)} method\n * supports in-place record filtering for log compaction and tombstone deletion. This method handles batch\n * rebuilding with updated metadata as needed.\n *\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for building new record buffers\n * @see RecordBatch for individual batch interface\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch for magic v2+ concrete implementation\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {",
  "oldString": "/**\n * A {@link Records} implementation backed by a ByteBuffer. This is used only for reading or\n * modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer see {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder},\n * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.KRaftVersionRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.LeaderChangeMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotFooterRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotHeaderRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.VotersRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransferableChannel;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetention;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetentionResult;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AbstractIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Arrays;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Objects;\n\n/**\n * A {@link Records} implementation backed by a ByteBuffer. This is used only for reading or\n * modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer see {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder},\n * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n    public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    // Construct a writable memory records\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return buffer.limit();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n        if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n                    + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n\n        return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\n     * @param channel The channel to write to\n     * @return The number of bytes written\n     * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\n     */\n    public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        buffer.mark();\n        int written = 0;\n        while (written < sizeInBytes())\n            written += channel.write(buffer);\n        buffer.reset();\n        return written;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * The total number of bytes in this message set not including any partial, trailing messages. This\n     * may be smaller than what is returned by {@link #sizeInBytes()}.\n     * @return The number of valid bytes\n     */\n    public int validBytes() {\n        if (validBytes >= 0)\n            return validBytes;\n\n        int bytes = 0;\n        for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n            bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n        this.validBytes = bytes;\n        return bytes;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n        return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Validates the header of the first batch and returns batch size.\n     * @return first batch size including LOG_OVERHEAD if buffer contains header up to\n     *         magic byte, null otherwise\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if record size or magic is invalid\n     */\n    public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n        if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n            return null;\n        return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Filter the records into the provided ByteBuffer.\n     *\n     * @param filter                      The filter function\n     * @param destinationBuffer           The byte buffer to write the filtered records to\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported. For small\n     *                                    record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4) will\n     *                                    dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return A FilterResult with a summary of the output (for metrics) and potentially an overflow buffer\n     */\n    public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\n     * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\n     */\n    private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\n                                         ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\n        for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\n            final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\n            final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\n\n            filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n            if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\n                continue;\n\n            final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\n                filter);\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\n            boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\n            long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\n\n            if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\n                // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\n                // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\n                // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\n                boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\n                    batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\n                if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\n                    batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\n                    filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\n                } else {\n                    long deleteHorizonMs;\n                    if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\n                        deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\n                    else\n                        deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\n                    try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\n                            bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\n                        MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\n                        int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\n                        MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\n                        filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\n                            maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\n                if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\");\n\n                bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n                DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\n                        batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\n                        batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\n                        batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n                filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\n            }\n\n            // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\n            // avoid the need for additional allocations.\n            ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\n            if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\n                filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n                return filterResult;\n            }\n        }\n\n        return filterResult;\n    }\n\n    private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\n                                                 BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\n                                                 FilterResult filterResult,\n                                                 RecordFilter filter) {\n        try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator =
