The reason for the different behavior is that there is\n        // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\n        TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\n                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\n        long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\n                originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\n                originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\n\n        // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\n        MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n                compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\n                originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\n                originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\n\n        for (Record record : retainedRecords)\n            builder.append(record);\n\n        if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\n            // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\n            // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\n            // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\n            builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\n\n        return builder;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\n     *\n     * <p>Returns a duplicate of the underlying buffer, so position and limit changes to the returned buffer\n     * do not affect the internal state of this MemoryRecords instance. However, the buffer shares the same\n     * backing byte array, so modifications to the buffer's content are visible.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Use Cases:</b>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Serializing records to a channel or output stream</li>\n     *   <li>Passing record data to other layers (e.g., network transport)</li>\n     *   <li>Direct memory operations requiring ByteBuffer manipulation</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * @return A duplicate of the underlying buffer. The position is at the start of the record data.\n     *         The limit is at the end of the record data.\n     * @see #slice(int, int) for creating a view of a portion of the records\n     */\n    public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n        return buffer.duplicate();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a new MemoryRecords instance representing a slice of this buffer.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Memory Efficiency:</b> The returned MemoryRecords shares the underlying byte array with the original\n     * buffer but provides a view starting at the specified position. This is a zero-copy operation that does not\n     * allocate new memory for the record data itself (though a new ByteBuffer view is created).\n     *\n     * <p><b>Position and Limit:</b> The returned MemoryRecords will iterate over records starting at the given\n     * position. If the actual available bytes are less than the requested size, the available bytes are used.\n     * The slice must represent complete record batches; partial batches at the boundaries may be skipped\n     * by the batch iterator.\n     *\n     * @param position The byte offset in this buffer to start the slice (0-based, relative to buffer start)\n     * @param size The maximum number of bytes to include in the slice. Actual slice size may be smaller\n     *             if fewer bytes are available.\n     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance representing the sliced region. Modifications to the original\n     *         buffer are visible through the slice (and vice versa).\n     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if position is negative, exceeds buffer limit, or size is negative\n     * @see #buffer() for accessing the underlying ByteBuffer\n     */\n    @Override\n    public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\n        if (position < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid position: \" + position + \" in read from \" + this);\n        if (position > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Slice from position \" + position + \" exceeds end position of \" + this);\n        if (size < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid size: \" + size + \" in read from \" + this);\n\n        int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\n        // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\n        // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\n        // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\n        // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\n        // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\n        // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\n        ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\n        slicedBuffer.position(position);\n        slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\n        // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\n        slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\n\n        return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n        return batches;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"MemoryRecords(size=\" + sizeInBytes() +\n                \", buffer=\" + buffer +\n                \")\";\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n        if (this == o)\n            return true;\n        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n            return false;\n\n        MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\n\n        return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int hashCode() {\n        return buffer.hashCode();\n    }\n\n    public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n        public final long currentTime;\n        public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n\n        public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n            this.currentTime = currentTime;\n            this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n        }\n\n        public static class BatchRetentionResult {\n            public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\n            public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\n                                        final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\n                this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\n                this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            }\n        }\n\n        public enum BatchRetention {\n            DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\n            RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\n            DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\n        }\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\n         * check the records individually).\n         */\n        protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\n         * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\n         * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\n         */\n        protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\n    }\n\n    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(MutableRecordBatch retainedBatch, int numMessagesInBatch, boolean headerOnly) {\n            int bytesRetained = headerOnly ? DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD : retainedBatch.sizeInBytes();\n            updateRetainedBatchMetadata(retainedBatch.maxTimestamp(), retainedBatch.lastOffset(),\n                    retainedBatch.lastOffset(), numMessagesInBatch, bytesRetained);\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset,\n                                                 int messagesRetained, int bytesRetained) {\n            validateBatchMetadata(maxTimestamp, shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, maxOffset);\n            if (maxTimestamp > this.maxTimestamp) {\n                this.maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp;\n                this.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n            }\n            this.maxOffset = Math.max(maxOffset, this.maxOffset);\n            this.messagesRetained += messagesRetained;\n            this.bytesRetained += bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        private void validateBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset) {\n            if (maxTimestamp != RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP && shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"shallowOffset undefined for maximum timestamp \" + maxTimestamp);\n            if (maxOffset < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"maxOffset undefined\");\n        }\n\n        public ByteBuffer outputBuffer() {\n            return outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRead() {\n            return messagesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRead() {\n            return bytesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRetained() {\n            return messagesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRetained() {\n            return bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public long maxOffset() {\n            return maxOffset;\n        }\n\n        public long maxTimestamp() {\n            return maxTimestamp;\n        }\n\n        public long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n            return shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a MemoryRecords instance for reading from the given buffer.\n     *\n     * <p>This is the primary factory method for creating readable MemoryRecords instances. The buffer should\n     * contain serialized {@link RecordBatch} structures. To create writable (mutable) MemoryRecords with new\n     * batches, use {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} instead.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Thread-Safety:</b> The returned instance is not thread-safe. Concurrent access to the underlying\n     * buffer will cause undefined behavior.\n     *\n     * @param buffer A ByteBuffer containing serialized record batches. The buffer position and limit determine\n     *               the readable region. The position should be at the start of valid record data.\n     * @return A MemoryRecords instance wrapping the buffer\n     * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for creating new record batches\n     * @see #buffer() for accessing the underlying ByteBuffer from a MemoryRecords instance\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecords readableRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        return new MemoryRecords(buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a builder for writing record batches to the given buffer with default parameters.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Defaults:</b>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Magic: {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE} (magic v2)</li>\n     *   <li>Log append time: Current system time if timestampType is LOG_APPEND_TIME, otherwise NO_TIMESTAMP</li>\n     *   <li>Producer ID: {@link RecordBatch#NO_PRODUCER_ID} (no idempotence/transactions)</li>\n     *   <li>Transactional: false</li>\n     *   <li>Control batch: false</li>\n     *   <li>Partition leader epoch: {@link RecordBatch#NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH}</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Use Case:</b> Simple producers that don't require idempotence or transactions.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer to write batches to. Must have sufficient capacity.\n     * @param compression The compression algorithm to use for records in batches\n     * @param timestampType CREATE_TIME or LOG_APPEND_TIME. If LOG_APPEND_TIME, the current system time is used.\n     * @param baseOffset The offset of the first record in the first batch\n     * @return A MemoryRecordsBuilder for appending records\n     * @see #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long) for more control over parameters\n     * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for the full builder interface\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset) {\n        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               int maxSize) {\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n\n        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,\n            logAppendTime, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,\n            false, false, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, maxSize);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset) {\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime,\n                                               int partitionLeaderEpoch) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long producerId,\n                                               short producerEpoch,\n                                               int baseSequence,\n                                               boolean isTransactional) {\n        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, baseOffset,\n                RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime,\n                                               long producerId,\n                                               short producerEpoch,\n                                               int baseSequence) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, false, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime,\n                                               long producerId,\n                                               short producerEpoch,\n                                               int baseSequence,\n                                               boolean isTransactional,\n                                               int partitionLeaderEpoch) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,\n                logAppendTime, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, false, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime,\n                                               long producerId,\n                                               short producerEpoch,\n                                               int baseSequence,\n                                               boolean isTransactional,\n                                               boolean isControlBatch,\n                                               int partitionLeaderEpoch) {\n        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,\n                logAppendTime, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, isControlBatch, partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                buffer.remaining());\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a MemoryRecords instance containing the given records in a single batch.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Batch Parameters:</b>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Magic: {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE} (magic v2)</li>\n     *   <li>Base offset: 0</li>\n     *   <li>Timestamp type: CREATE_TIME</li>\n     *   <li>Producer ID: {@link RecordBatch#NO_PRODUCER_ID}</li>\n     *   <li>Transactional: false</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Use Case:</b> Convenient factory for creating test data or simple record sets. For production use,\n     * consider using {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} for more control over batch parameters.\n     *\n     * @param compression The compression algorithm to apply to records in the batch\n     * @param records The records to include. Empty records array creates an empty MemoryRecords instance.\n     * @return A MemoryRecords instance containing the records in a single batch\n     * @see #withIdempotentRecords(Compression, long, short, int, SimpleRecord...) for idempotent producers\n     * @see #withTransactionalRecords(Compression, long, short, int, SimpleRecord...) for transactional producers\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(Compression compression, int partitionLeaderEpoch, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,\n                partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(long initialOffset, Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(long initialOffset, Compression compression, int partitionLeaderEpoch, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                      short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,\n                baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                                      long producerId, short producerEpoch, int baseSequence,\n                                                      int partitionLeaderEpoch, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,\n                baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(long initialOffset, Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                      short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                                      SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, true, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                                         long producerId, short producerEpoch, int baseSequence,\n                                                         int partitionLeaderEpoch, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,\n                baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, true, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(long initialOffset, Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                                         SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withTransactionalRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                            TimestampType timestampType, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, timestampType, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH,\n                false, records);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                            TimestampType timestampType, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                            int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch, boolean isTransactional,\n                                            SimpleRecord... records) {\n        if (records.length == 0)\n            return MemoryRecords.EMPTY;\n        int sizeEstimate = AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytes(magic, compression.type(), Arrays.asList(records));\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(sizeEstimate);\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n        try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferStream, magic, compression, timestampType,\n                initialOffset, logAppendTime, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, false,\n                partitionLeaderEpoch, sizeEstimate)) {\n            for (SimpleRecord record : records)\n                builder.append(record);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long producerId, short producerEpoch, EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        return withEndTransactionMarker(0L, System.currentTimeMillis(), RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, marker);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long timestamp, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                                         EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        return withEndTransactionMarker(0L, timestamp, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, producerId,\n                producerEpoch, marker);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long initialOffset, long timestamp, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                                         long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                                         EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        int endTxnMarkerBatchSize = DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + marker.endTxnMarkerValueSize();\n        ByteBuffer buffer = ByteBuffer.allocate(endTxnMarkerBatchSize);\n        writeEndTransactionalMarker(buffer, initialOffset, timestamp, partitionLeaderEpoch, producerId,\n                producerEpoch, marker);\n        buffer.flip();\n        return MemoryRecords.readableRecords(buffer);\n    }\n\n    public static void writeEndTransactionalMarker(ByteBuffer buffer, long initialOffset, long timestamp,\n                                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                                   EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        boolean isTransactional = true;\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, Compression.NONE,\n                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, initialOffset, timestamp, producerId, producerEpoch,\n                RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, isTransactional, true, partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                buffer.capacity())\n        ) {\n            builder.appendEndTxnMarker(timestamp, marker);\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withLeaderChangeMessage(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer,\n        LeaderChangeMessage leaderChangeMessage\n    ) {\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n                initialOffset,\n                timestamp,\n                leaderEpoch,\n                buffer\n            )\n        ) {\n            builder.appendLeaderChangeMessage(timestamp, leaderChangeMessage);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withSnapshotHeaderRecord(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer,\n        SnapshotHeaderRecord snapshotHeaderRecord\n    ) {\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n                initialOffset,\n                timestamp,\n                leaderEpoch,\n                buffer\n            )\n        ) {\n            builder.appendSnapshotHeaderMessage(timestamp, snapshotHeaderRecord);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withSnapshotFooterRecord(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer,\n        SnapshotFooterRecord snapshotFooterRecord\n    ) {\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n                initialOffset,\n                timestamp,\n                leaderEpoch,\n                buffer\n            )\n        ) {\n            builder.appendSnapshotFooterMessage(timestamp, snapshotFooterRecord);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withKRaftVersionRecord(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer,\n        KRaftVersionRecord kraftVersionRecord\n    ) {\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n                initialOffset,\n                timestamp,\n                leaderEpoch,\n                buffer\n            )\n        ) {\n            builder.appendKRaftVersionMessage(timestamp, kraftVersionRecord);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withVotersRecord(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer,\n        VotersRecord votersRecord\n    ) {\n        try (MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n                initialOffset,\n                timestamp,\n                leaderEpoch,\n                buffer\n            )\n        ) {\n            builder.appendVotersMessage(timestamp, votersRecord);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static MemoryRecordsBuilder createKraftControlRecordBuilder(\n        long initialOffset,\n        long timestamp,\n        int leaderEpoch,\n        ByteBuffer buffer\n    ) {\n        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(\n            buffer,\n            RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n            Compression.NONE,\n            TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n            initialOffset,\n            timestamp,\n            RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID,\n            RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH,\n            RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,\n            false,\n            true,\n            leaderEpoch,\n            buffer.capacity()\n        );\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "                 RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);",
        "     }",
        " ",
        "+    /**",
        "+     * Creates a MemoryRecords instance with records from an idempotent producer.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p>Idempotent producers use sequence numbers and producer ID/epoch to detect and eliminate duplicates.",
        "+     * This method creates a batch with these fields set appropriately for idempotence.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p><b>Batch Parameters:</b>",
        "+     * <ul>",
        "+     *   <li>Magic: {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE} (magic v2)</li>",
        "+     *   <li>Base offset: 0</li>",
        "+     *   <li>Timestamp type: CREATE_TIME</li>",
        "+     *   <li>Partition leader epoch: {@link RecordBatch#NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH}</li>",
        "+     *   <li>Transactional: false</li>",
        "+     * </ul>",
        "+     *",
        "+     * <p><b>Producer State:</b> The producer ID and epoch must be assigned by the broker and are used to",
        "+     * maintain producer state for duplicate detection. Sequence numbers must be unique within a producer.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * @param compression The compression algorithm for records",
        "+     * @param producerId The ID of the producer (assigned by broker)",
        "+     * @param producerEpoch The epoch of the producer (incremented on broker restart)",
        "+     * @param baseSequence The base sequence number for the first record (0-based)",
        "+     * @param records The records to include",
        "+     * @return A MemoryRecords instance with idempotent producer metadata",
        "+     * @see #withTransactionalRecords(Compression, long, short, int, SimpleRecord...) for transactional producers",
        "+     * @see #withRecords(Compression, SimpleRecord...) for non-idempotent producers",
        "+     */",
        "     public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,",
        "                                                       short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {",
        "         return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,"
      ],
      "newLines": 33,
      "newStart": 789,
      "oldLines": 6,
      "oldStart": 789
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}
31. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java",
  "new_string": "    /**\n     * Creates a MemoryRecords instance with records from a transactional producer.\n     *\n     * <p>Transactional batches are part of multi-partition transactions managed by the broker. They contain\n     * application records that may be rolled back or committed as an atomic unit. This method creates batches\n     * with the transactional flag set.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Batch Parameters:</b>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Magic: {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE} (magic v2)</li>\n     *   <li>Base offset: 0</li>\n     *   <li>Timestamp type: CREATE_TIME</li>\n     *   <li>Partition leader epoch: {@link RecordBatch#NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH}</li>\n     *   <li>Transactional: true</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Transaction Markers:</b> In addition to application records, transactions also include control records\n     * (commit/abort markers) that are added separately via {@link #withEndTransactionMarker(long, long, short, EndTransactionMarker)}.\n     *\n     * @param compression The compression algorithm for records\n     * @param producerId The ID of the producer (assigned by broker)\n     * @param producerEpoch The epoch of the producer\n     * @param baseSequence The base sequence number for the first record\n     * @param records The records to include in this transaction batch\n     * @return A MemoryRecords instance with transactional metadata\n     * @see #withEndTransactionMarker(long, long, short, EndTransactionMarker) for transaction markers\n     * @see #withIdempotentRecords(Compression, long, short, int, SimpleRecord...) for idempotent non-transactional\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, true, records);\n    }",
  "old_string": "    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, true, records);\n    }",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Creates a MemoryRecords instance with records from a transactional producer.\n     *\n     * <p>Transactional batches are part of multi-partition transactions managed by the broker. They contain\n     * application records that may be rolled back or committed as an atomic unit. This method creates batches\n     * with the transactional flag set.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Batch Parameters:</b>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Magic: {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE} (magic v2)</li>\n     *   <li>Base offset: 0</li>\n     *   <li>Timestamp type: CREATE_TIME</li>\n     *   <li>Partition leader epoch: {@link RecordBatch#NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH}</li>\n     *   <li>Transactional: true</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Transaction Markers:</b> In addition to application records, transactions also include control records\n     * (commit/abort markers) that are added separately via {@link #withEndTransactionMarker(long, long, short, EndTransactionMarker)}.\n     *\n     * @param compression The compression algorithm for records\n     * @param producerId The ID of the producer (assigned by broker)\n     * @param producerEpoch The epoch of the producer\n     * @param baseSequence The base sequence number for the first record\n     * @param records The records to include in this transaction batch\n     * @return A MemoryRecords instance with transactional metadata\n     * @see #withEndTransactionMarker(long, long, short, EndTransactionMarker) for transaction markers\n     * @see #withIdempotentRecords(Compression, long, short, int, SimpleRecord...) for idempotent non-transactional\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, true, records);\n    }",
  "oldString": "    public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,\n                                                         short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {\n        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, true, records);\n    }",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.KRaftVersionRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.LeaderChangeMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotFooterRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotHeaderRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.VotersRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransferableChannel;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetention;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetentionResult;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AbstractIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Arrays;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Objects;\n\n/**\n * A {@link Records} implementation backed by a ByteBuffer. This is used for reading, writing, and modifying\n * in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer, use {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder}\n * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> MemoryRecords is the primary in-memory representation of serialized Kafka\n * record batches. It provides methods for iterating over batches, filtering records by retention policies,\n * and serializing to various output formats (ByteBuffer, channel, output stream).\n *\n * <p><b>Thread-Safety:</b> This class is not thread-safe. The underlying ByteBuffer is shared with callers, and\n * concurrent modification (e.g., changing the buffer position or limit) will cause undefined behavior. Use external\n * synchronization or create separate instances for concurrent access.\n *\n * <p><b>Memory Efficiency:</b> MemoryRecords provides lazy iteration over batches and lazy decompression of\n * records via {@link RecordBatch#streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}. This allows for processing large record\n * sets with bounded memory usage.\n *\n * <p><b>Filtering and Compaction:</b> The {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)} method\n * supports in-place record filtering for log compaction and tombstone deletion. This method handles batch\n * rebuilding with updated metadata as needed.\n *\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for building new record buffers\n * @see RecordBatch for individual batch interface\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch for magic v2+ concrete implementation\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n    public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a MemoryRecords instance wrapping the given buffer.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Thread-Safety:</b> This class is not thread-safe. Concurrent access to the buffer (including position\n     * and limit modifications) will result in undefined behavior. The caller retains ownership of the buffer.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Buffer Semantics:</b> The buffer should be positioned at the start of the record data and contain\n     * complete {@link RecordBatch} structures. The limit determines the end of valid record data.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer containing serialized record batches. Must not be null.\n     * @throws NullPointerException if buffer is null\n     */\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return buffer.limit();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n        if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n                    + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n\n        return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\n     * @param channel The channel to write to\n     * @return The number of bytes written\n     * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\n     */\n    public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        buffer.mark();\n        int written = 0;\n        while (written < sizeInBytes())\n            written += channel.write(buffer);\n        buffer.reset();\n        return written;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * The total number of bytes in this message set not including any partial, trailing messages.\n     * This may be smaller than what is returned by {@link #sizeInBytes()}.\n     *\n     * <p>Valid bytes are computed by iterating through all batches and summing their sizes. Trailing partial\n     * batches (incomplete due to network truncation or buffer size limits) are excluded from this count.\n     * This is useful for determining how much of the buffer contains complete, valid batch data.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance Note:</b> This method caches the result after the first call. If the underlying buffer\n     * is modified, the cached value is not invalidated; callers must ensure they do not modify the buffer\n     * after calling this method or create a new MemoryRecords instance.\n     *\n     * @return The number of bytes containing complete, valid batches. May be less than {@link #sizeInBytes()}\n     *         if partial batches are present.\n     * @see #sizeInBytes() for the total buffer size including partial batches\n     */\n    public int validBytes() {\n        if (validBytes >= 0)\n            return validBytes;\n\n        int bytes = 0;\n        for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n            bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n        this.validBytes = bytes;\n        return bytes;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n        return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Validates the header of the first batch and returns its size.\n     *\n     * <p>This method checks if the buffer contains sufficient data for reading a complete batch header\n     * (up to and including the magic byte and batch size field). It then parses and returns the size\n     * of the first batch. This is useful for incremental batch reading and validation.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Partial Batch Handling:</b> If the buffer contains less than {@link AbstractRecords#HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC}\n     * bytes, null is returned to indicate that the batch header is incomplete. The caller can wait for more data\n     * before retrying.\n     *\n     * @return The size of the first batch including {@link AbstractRecords#LOG_OVERHEAD} bytes if the buffer\n     *         contains a complete header, or null if the header is incomplete or the buffer is empty.\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if the batch size field is invalid (e.g., negative or exceeds reasonable limits)\n     *                                  or if the magic byte is invalid\n     * @see AbstractRecords#LOG_OVERHEAD for the size overhead\n     * @see AbstractRecords#HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC for the minimum header size needed\n     */\n    public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n        if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n            return null;\n        return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Filters records according to a {@link RecordFilter} and writes the filtered result to a ByteBuffer.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Filtering Process:</b> This method iterates through all batches, applying batch-level retention logic,\n     * then record-level retention logic. Batches and records that fail the filter are dropped. If a batch is partially\n     * filtered (some records retained, some dropped), the batch is rebuilt with updated metadata. This includes:\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Recompressing the filtered records if the original batch was compressed</li>\n     *   <li>Updating delete horizon timestamps if tombstones are present</li>\n     *   <li>Preserving base and last offset information for producer state recovery</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> Memory usage depends on buffer supplier caching:\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>With {@link BufferSupplier#GrowableBufferSupplier}: Decompression buffers are reused across batches</li>\n     *   <li>With {@link BufferSupplier#NO_CACHING}: New buffers allocated for each compressed batch (higher GC pressure)</li>\n     * </ul>\n     * For large compressed batches, using a reusable supplier is strongly recommended.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Output Buffer Management:</b> If the filtered records do not fit in the provided destinationBuffer,\n     * a new buffer is allocated and returned via {@link FilterResult#outputBuffer()}. Callers should check for\n     * buffer overflow and handle it appropriately (e.g., re-attempt with larger buffer or chain filters).\n     *\n     * @param filter The filter logic to apply. Implementers should override\n     *               {@link RecordFilter#checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)} and\n     *               {@link RecordFilter#shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch, Record)}.\n     * @param destinationBuffer The ByteBuffer to write filtered records to. Must be writable with remaining capacity\n     *                          for the filtered output. If insufficient, a new buffer is allocated.\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression. For optimal performance\n     *                                    with multiple compressed batches, use a supplier that reuses buffers\n     *                                    (e.g., {@link BufferSupplier#GrowableBufferSupplier}).\n     *                                    For small record batches, buffer allocation dominates CPU cost.\n     * @return A {@link FilterResult} containing filtered metadata (messages read/retained, bytes read/retained, max offset/timestamp)\n     *         and the output buffer (which may differ from destinationBuffer if overflow occurred).\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if a batch or record is corrupt\n     * @throws InvalidRecordException if record decoding fails\n     * @see RecordFilter for filter implementation details\n     * @see FilterResult for result interpretation\n     */\n    public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\n     * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\n     */\n    private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\n                                         ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\n        for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\n            final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\n            final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\n\n            filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n            if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\n                continue;\n\n            final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\n                filter);\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\n            boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\n            long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\n\n            if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\n                // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\n                // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\n                // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\n                boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\n                    batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\n                if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\n                    batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\n                    filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\n                } else {\n                    long deleteHorizonMs;\n                    if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\n                        deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\n                    else\n                        deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\n                    try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\n                            bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\n                        MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\n                        int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\n                        MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\n                        filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\n                            maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\n                if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\");\n\n                bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n                DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\n                        batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\n                        batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\n                        batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n                filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\n            }\n\n            // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\n            // avoid the need for additional allocations.\n            ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\n            if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\n                filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n                return filterResult;\n            }\n        }\n\n        return filterResult;\n    }\n\n    private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\n                                                 BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\n                                                 FilterResult filterResult,\n                                                 RecordFilter filter) {\n        try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\n            long maxOffset = -1;\n            boolean containsTombstones = false;\n            // Convert records with old record versions\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\n            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iterator.next();\n                filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\n\n                if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\n                    if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\n                        maxOffset = record.offset();\n\n                    retainedRecords.add(record);\n\n                    if (!record.hasValue()) {\n                        containsTombstones = true;\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    writeOriginalBatch = false;\n                }\n            }\n            return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class BatchFilterResult {\n        private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\n        private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\n        private final boolean containsTombstones;\n        private final long maxOffset;\n        private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                  final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\n                                  final boolean containsTombstones,\n                                  final long maxOffset) {\n            this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\n            this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\n            this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\n            this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\n                                                                 List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                                                 ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\n                                                                 final long deleteHorizonMs) {\n        Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\n        // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\n        // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\n        // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\n        // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\n        TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\n                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\n        long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\n                originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\n                originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\n\n        // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\n        MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n                compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\n                originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\n                originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\n\n        for (Record record : retainedRecords)\n            builder.append(record);\n\n        if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\n            // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\n            // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\n            // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\n            builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\n\n        return builder;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\n     *\n     * <p>Returns a duplicate of the underlying buffer, so position and limit changes to the returned buffer\n     * do not affect the internal state of this MemoryRecords instance. However, the buffer shares the same\n     * backing byte array, so modifications to the buffer's content are visible.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Use Cases:</b>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Serializing records to a channel or output stream</li>\n     *   <li>Passing record data to other layers (e.g., network transport)</li>\n     *   <li>Direct memory operations requiring ByteBuffer manipulation</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * @return A duplicate of the underlying buffer. The position is at the start of the record data.\n     *         The limit is at the end of the record data.\n     * @see #slice(int, int) for creating a view of a portion of the records\n     */\n    public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n        return buffer.duplicate();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a new MemoryRecords instance representing a slice of this buffer.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Memory Efficiency:</b> The returned MemoryRecords shares the underlying byte array with the original\n     * buffer but provides a view starting at the specified position. This is a zero-copy operation that does not\n     * allocate new memory for the record data itself (though a new ByteBuffer view is created).\n     *\n     * <p><b>Position and Limit:</b> The returned MemoryRecords will iterate over records starting at the given\n     * position. If the actual available bytes are less than the requested size, the available bytes are used.\n     * The slice must represent complete record batches; partial batches at the boundaries may be skipped\n     * by the batch iterator.\n     *\n     * @param position The byte offset in this buffer to start the slice (0-based, relative to buffer start)\n     * @param size The maximum number of bytes to include in the slice. Actual slice size may be smaller\n     *             if fewer bytes are available.\n     * @return A new MemoryRecords instance representing the sliced region. Modifications to the original\n     *         buffer are visible through the slice (and vice versa).\n     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if position is negative, exceeds buffer limit, or size is negative\n     * @see #buffer() for accessing the underlying ByteBuffer\n     */\n    @Override\n    public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\n        if (position < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid position: \" + position + \" in read from \" + this);\n        if (position > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Slice from position \" + position + \" exceeds end position of \" + this);\n        if (size < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid size: \" + size + \" in read from \" + this);\n\n        int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\n        // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\n        // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\n        // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\n        // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\n        // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\n        // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\n        ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\n        slicedBuffer.position(position);\n        slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\n        // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\n        slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\n\n        return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n        return batches;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"MemoryRecords(size=\" + sizeInBytes() +\n                \", buffer=\" + buffer +\n                \")\";\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n        if (this == o)\n            return true;\n        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n            return false;\n\n        MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\n\n        return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int hashCode() {\n        return buffer.hashCode();\n    }\n\n    public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n        public final long currentTime;\n        public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n\n        public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n            this.currentTime = currentTime;\n            this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n        }\n\n        public static class BatchRetentionResult {\n            public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\n            public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\n                                        final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\n                this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\n                this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            }\n        }\n\n        public enum BatchRetention {\n            DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\n            RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\n            DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\n        }\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\n         * check the records individually).\n         */\n        protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\n         * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\n         * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\n         */\n        protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\n    }\n\n    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(MutableRecordBatch retainedBatch, int numMessagesInBatch, boolean headerOnly) {\n            int bytesRetained = headerOnly ? DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD : retainedBatch.sizeInBytes();\n            updateRetainedBatchMetadata(retainedBatch.maxTimestamp(), retainedBatch.lastOffset(),\n                    retainedBatch.lastOffset(), numMessagesInBatch, bytesRetained);\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset,\n                                                 int messagesRetained, int bytesRetained) {\n            validateBatchMetadata(maxTimestamp, shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, maxOffset);\n            if (maxTimestamp > this.maxTimestamp) {\n                this.maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp;\n                this.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n            }\n            this.maxOffset = Math.max(maxOffset, this.maxOffset);\n            this.messagesRetained += messagesRetained;\n            this.bytesRetained += bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        private void validateBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset) {\n            if (maxTimestamp != RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP && shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"shallowOffset undefined for maximum timestamp \" + maxTimestamp);\n            if (maxOffset < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"maxOffset undefined\");\n        }\n\n        public ByteBuffer outputBuffer() {\n            return outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRead() {\n            return messagesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRead() {\n            return bytesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRetained() {\n            return messagesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRetained() {\n            return bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public long maxOffset() {\n            return maxOffset;\n        }\n\n        public long maxTimestamp() {\n            return maxTimestamp;\n        }\n\n        public long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n            return shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a MemoryRecords instance for reading from the given buffer.\n     *\n     * <p>This is the primary factory method for creating readable MemoryRecords instances. The buffer should\n     * contain serialized {@link RecordBatch} structures. To create writable (mutable) MemoryRecords with new\n     * batches, use {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} instead.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Thread-Safety:</b> The returned instance is not thread-safe. Concurrent access to the underlying\n     * buffer will cause undefined behavior.\n     *\n     * @param buffer A ByteBuffer containing serialized record batches. The buffer position and limit determine\n     *               the readable region. The position should be at the start of valid record data.\n     * @return A MemoryRecords instance wrapping the buffer\n     * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for creating new record batches\n     * @see #buffer() for accessing the underlying ByteBuffer from a MemoryRecords instance\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecords readableRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        return new MemoryRecords(buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Creates a builder for writing record batches to the given buffer with default parameters.\n     *\n     * <p><b>Defaults:</b>\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Magic: {@link RecordBatch#CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE} (magic v2)</li>\n     *   <li>Log append time: Current system time if timestampType is LOG_APPEND_TIME, otherwise NO_TIMESTAMP</li>\n     *   <li>Producer ID: {@link RecordBatch#NO_PRODUCER_ID} (no idempotence/transactions)</li>\n     *   <li>Transactional: false</li>\n     *   <li>Control batch: false</li>\n     *   <li>Partition leader epoch: {@link RecordBatch#NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH}</li>\n     * </ul>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Use Case:</b> Simple producers that don't require idempotence or transactions.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The ByteBuffer to write batches to. Must have sufficient capacity.\n     * @param compression The compression algorithm to use for records in batches\n     * @param timestampType CREATE_TIME or LOG_APPEND_TIME. If LOG_APPEND_TIME, the current system time is used.\n     * @param baseOffset The offset of the first record in the first batch\n     * @return A MemoryRecordsBuilder for appending records\n     * @see #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long, long) for more control over parameters\n     * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for the full builder interface\n     */\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset) {\n        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               int maxSize) {\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n\n        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,\n            logAppendTime, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,\n            false, false, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, maxSize);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset) {\n        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long logAppendTime,\n                                               int partitionLeaderEpoch) {\n        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               long baseOffset,\n                                               long producerId,\n                                               short producerEpoch,\n                                               int baseSequence,\n                                               boolean isTransactional) {\n        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, baseOffset,\n                RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional,\n                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n
